MINI MINUTES: MOTION FOR AN ORDER DEEMING THE TRUTH OF THE MATTERS SPECIFIED IN PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS AS ADMITTED

Appearances: Jonathan Vaknin (415.851.5297) specially appearing for plaintiff and moving party; No other appearances.

This matter is not argued. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Wells Fargo's motion for an order deeming the truth of the matters specified in plaintiff's request for admissions is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. Order signed. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Pursuant To Ccp437c
Off Calendar May-09-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Pursuant To Ccp437c.

The unopposed motion for summary judgment is denied as untimely served. Wells Fargo's proof of service shows that service was made by mail within California on February 19, 2025, 79 days before the hearing. CCP 437c(a)(2) provides, "Notice of the motion and supporting papers shall be served on all other parties to the action at least 81 days before the time appointed for hearing. If the notice is served by mail, the required 81-day period of notice shall be increased by 5 days if the place of address is within the State of California . . ."

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
